calendar.matters:
Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stip Or/In The Alternative To Set Aside The Stip For Judgment And Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jun-05-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Mercedes Gavin, Esq., 464-9841, for plaintiff The John Stewart Co. moving for entry of judgment pursuant to stipulation or/in the alternative to set aside the stipulation for judgment and settlement agreement
Ora S. Prochovnick, Esq., Supervising Attorney and Dino A. Duazo, Certified Legal Intern of HOUSING ADVOCACY CLINIC, NEW COLLEGE OF CALIFORNIA 982-8399, for defendant and opposing party

RULING:
PLAINTIFF THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Stip Or/In The Alternative To Set Aside The Stip For Judgment And Settlement Agreement -- Motion was treated as a motion for reconsideration to Court's previous ruling and argued before the Court. Matter was taken under submission. (DAG/301/SL) Reporter: Joe Vickstein, CSR 4780

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-05-2003 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for hearing this day. Judge: Ollie Marie-Victoire, Clerk: P. Dias, Reporter: Floyd Butler. Mercedes A. Gavin, Esq. represented The John Stewart Company and Ora Shelee Prochovnick, Esq. represented Defendant Tenecia Gippson-Kent. Also present, Donetta Ingram, Manager, representing the plaintiff. The case did not settle and the trial date is confirmed.

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Feb-11-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Dino Duazo, Certified Legal Intern and Superving Attorney of Housing Advocacy Clinic 982-8399, for defendant Tenecia Gippson-Kent moving to set aside default/default judgment/leave to defend
Mercedes Gavin, Esq., 464-9841, for plaintiff and opposing party

RULING:
A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling -- DEFENDANT TENECIA GIPPSON-KENT's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND -- GRANTED -- no opposition filed. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (DAG/301/JU) Reporter: Joseph Vickstein, CSR 4780

Motion Granting Relief From Forfeiture Due To Hardship
Off Calendar Feb-11-2003 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Dino Duazo, Certified Legal Intern and Superving Attorney of Housing Advocacy Clinic 982-8399, for defendant Tenecia Gippson-Kent moving for a relief from fortfeiture due to hardship
Mercedes Gavin, Esq., 464-9841, for plaintiff and opposing party

RULING:
A R G U E D and the Court took DEFENDANT TENECIA GIPPSON-KENT's Motion Granting Relief From Forfeiture Due To Hardship off calendar, matter is moot. (DAG/301/JU) Reporter: Joseph Vickstein, CSR 4780
